other_observers: Brandon Percival and Mark Peterson; Brandon Percival found this bird on 13 Oct with Jeannie Mitchell and Kelly Shipe
duration: 15 minutes, 5 from a very distant view and 10 minutes
specific_location: Pueblo State Wildlife Area, west end of Pueblo Reservoir
description: Dorsally brownish jaeger with hooked bill and webbed feet. Head noticeably light gray colored contrasting with a broad browinsh throat band. Tail somewhat broad and rounded projecting past folded wingtips (see photo). Breast and belly pale whitish. Wings dark grayish with 2-3 pale primary shafts (see flight photo). Overall cold coloration without any warm rufous colors on any parts. Very broad and and noticeable dark barring on undertail. Back dark with noticeable lighter bars or feather edges creating somewhat of a two-toned appearance (see flight photo). Nothing nearby for comparison but it looked small. Bil with a dark tip, maybe 1/3 of the length.Behaviors: Loafed on water and flew with buoyant flight circling a large part of a very large reservoir. Said to have been eating small shad (Brandon Percival), but I did not observe this.Call: none heardPlumage: juvenile
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
